summary_text: 1. **High — Core safety promises are text-only, not runtime-enforced.** `x-auto-post-guard` declares mandatory preflight, quote-URL exclusion, proper-noun leakage checks, and publish gating, but repo tests only assert that those sentences exist; they do not verify behavior. The execution adapter for draft generation does not run those checks before writing candidates. Refs: [skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md#L110), [tests/test-x-auto-skill-contract.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-x-auto-skill-contract.sh#L17), [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh#L81) 2. **High — Draft-generation authority is delegated to out-of-repo code, reducing auditability.** The core generator is `~/Dev/x-auto/scripts/generate_draft_candidates.py`, outside this repo and its CI/test boundary. That means contract compliance cannot be proven from repository-visible evidence alone. Refs: [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh#L10), [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh#L12) 3. **Medium — Runtime-source-of-truth policy and health-check fallback are in tension.** Contract says verify active runtime from current checkout and avoid legacy-path assumptions, but health resolution still falls back to `~/Dev/x-auto` and `~/Documents/x-auto` and defaults to `~/Dev/x-auto` if no candidate matches. This can hide drift in multi-runtime setups. Refs: [docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md#L57), [scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L90), [scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L104) 4. **Medium — Hardcoded absolute paths create portability and environment-coupling risk.** Thumbnail sync and generation paths are tied to one local filesystem/user layout. This weakens reproducibility and can silently break on other hosts/sessions. Refs: [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-sync-approved-thumbnail.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-sync-approved-thumbnail.py#L14), [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-thumbnail-gen.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-thumbnail-gen.js#L11) 5. **Medium — Length policy is not strongly enforced for schedulable candidates.** Contract/skill says schedulable drafts should target `800-1500字`, but adapter validation allows any `min_chars >= 1`, with no explicit upper-bound or mandatory non-posting label for shorter ideation drafts. Refs: [docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md#L51), [skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md#L86), [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-draft-only.sh#L69) 6. **Low/Medium — Thumbnail diversity requirements are policy-only.** Skill requires at least 3 composition families when repetition is a concern, but generator runs one output per invocation and there is no enforcement test for “3-family minimum” or text-architecture variance in actual outputs. Refs: [skills/x-auto-thumbnail-art-director/SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-thumbnail-art-director/SKILL.md#L168), [scripts/local/integrations/xauto-thumbnail-gen.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/integrations/xauto-thumbnail-gen.js#L168), [tests/test-x-auto-skill-contract.sh]...
